scored_hypotheses.claim: The Voynich Manuscript encodes a late medieval Moravian (Old Czech/Slavic) dialect using a construct

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: The hypothesis is contradicted by statistical analysis: Voynich word-length (5.03) matches Romance languages, not Slavic; glyph entropy (3.8627) suggests non-natural language; the claim combining medieval Moravian with Gujarati plant names is implausible; and no consistent Moravian decipherment has been found despite extensive scholarship indicating constructed language or heavily abbreviated text instead.

scored_hypotheses.claim: The Voynich script is a semi-syllabic system derived from Balkan scribal traditions, where complex g

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: Statistics confirm natural-language-like properties (Zipfian distribution, hapax ratio) but provide no evidence for the specific Balkan syllabic system, proposed glyph values, or Serbo-Croatian word generation; hypothesis lacks empirical validation beyond speculation.

scored_hypotheses.claim: The Voynich Manuscript is a trilingual pharmaceutical compendium encoding plant-based medicine in a

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: Hypothesis contradicted: a hapax ratio of 0.701 indicates 70% of unique words appear only once, inconsistent with 168 terms covering 80% of text, which would require heavy concentration among those terms.
